TEST SETUP: There are three permissions categories on Bloom Library: Regular user, Collection Editor, and Moderator. To test, you will need an account from all three categories. The following presumes you have Collection Editor permission for the "Pratham" bookshelf.

Badges: Book thumbnail badges indicating Bloom Library status. There are four statuses seen here. 1) uploaded & harvested 2) waiting for harvester 3) uploaded as draft 4) uploaded and removed from publication. Completion of harvesting -- movement from State (2) to (1) or (2) to 3) -- will only be shown after re-booting Bloom. Likewise, state (4) is only detected after initial re-boot (or changing collections). Test bulk upload.

MY BOOKS: Sign in as a regular user, click on the top right avatar, and choose My Books. Click on any book. You should have see "You have permission to modify this book". Go to any book not in "My Books" --> the permissions are hidden.

DOWNLOAD INTO BLOOM FOR EDITING: When logged in as a Regular user, the "download for editing" button should only be available for "My Books" and no others; for a Collection Editor, the button is present for all books in the collection, or "My Books" (but no others); for a Moderator, all books have the button.

Click Download for Editing button. A new instance of Bloom will open. The thumbnail should show the downloaded badge. The "sources for new books" should be hidden. Collection settings should match the original settings of the uploaded book including Enterprise settings. The name of the collection should be "From Bloom Library - NAME OF BOOK". The branding should be maintained. You should not be able to create a new book in the collection.

Update Books without losing branding: make a book with any current project branding and upload it then download it for editing. A new instance of Bloom opens. Verify branding is maintained in the book. Add a new page and re-upload the updated book (tick "yes"). Verify branding is retained on Blorg and new page has been added. Add another page. Go to settings Enterprise and change the branding to None. Upload to web again. This time the "yes" option should require you to tick a box to acknowledge the branding is changing to None. Tick both and upload. Verify branding for the book on Blorg. Change branding to None and reupload. Verify you have to acknowledge the change in branding. Upload and verify result on Blorg.

Changing ownership: Log in as a Moderator and download a book for editing from the Pratham bookshelf which is not owned by either Moderator, Collection Editor, or Regular user. After Bloom opens, go to Publish to Web, tick agreements, and click upload. Under the "yes" option, the collision dialog should present tick box to change ownership. Don't upload. Close Bloom and log out as moderator and log in a Collection Editor. Open Bloom and Publish to Web again. The collision dialog should allow you to change ownership. Don't upload. Close Bloom and log out as Collection Editor and log in a Regular user. Open Bloom and Publish to Web again. The collision dialog should not present the option to change ownership and should not allow you to upload (i.e. after clicking "yes", the "upload" button should remain greyed out). Close Bloom and log out as Regular user and log in a Collection Editor. Open Bloom and Publish to Web again. The collision dialog should allow you to change ownership. Tick boxes and upload. Verify on Blorg that ownership has changed. Try re-uploading. This time the change-ownership box won't appear because owership has been transferred.

Text Languages: Ensure 1) all content languages are presented and your selection is remembered if you exit publish or switch between BloomPUB and Web Publish; 2) checkboxes are disabled and checked for any "required" languages regardless of completeness (e.g. a trilingual book will have three checked languages which are greyed out); 3) only the languages selected are uploaded (web) or embedded in saved BloomPUB; 4) tooltip says "Select this if you want readers to be able to choose to read the book in this language"; 5) if the book is bilingual or trilingual, then those languages cannot be unchecked regardless of completeness. 6) incomplete languages are correctly identified ; 7) a language which is only in xmatter is not advertised on blorg [Quick test resource: download this book in an English collection and publish. There should be English, French, Hausa, Spanish & Swahili.]

Talking Book Languages: Ensure 1) all audio languages embedded in the book are presented and are checked by default and your selection is remembered if you exit publish or switch between BloomPUB and Web Publish; 2) all boxes can be unchecked and; 3) tooltip says "Select this if you want to include this audio"; 4) the checkmark beside Features: Talking Book is checked iff there is one audio language checked. drag moves element: try all canvas element types, including navigation buttons; try at multiple zoom levels, including 100%, larger, and smaller. Red guide lines should appear when elements are aligned. Movement should snap to a grid (about 10px) unless ctrl is held down.

Again, things should snap to a grid unless ctrl is held down, and red alignment lines should show as appropriate; also green bars when the item being resized is the same height as something else. (Text bubbles are special and do not have corner handles.)
The width of a text bubble can be changed using the oval side handles. Normally the height adjusts automatically to fit the text.

Turn auto-height off using the ... menu below a text box. Now there should be a third handle in the middle bottom which allows the height to be manually adjusted. The box will no longer resize automatically as the side handles are moved, nor as text is typed or deleted.
Moving one corner of a bubble should not cause the opposite corner to move, even slightly. Compare BL-8077, though that was with an older resizing UI.

Images (but not currently image buttons) can be cropped using the handles on the sides. Try all four sides. The image should not move. As you drag outward, the handle should not move beyond the point where the image is no longer cropped. Watch out for rows of narrow lines being left behind during dragging, especially at zoom levels larger than 100%.

When an image is cropped, an extra handle appears, a circle in the middle of the image. This handle allows adjusting the cropping by moving the image, without changing the position or size of the containing box. It should not be possible to move the image beyond any of its edges (i.e., leaving white bars inside the box).
A cropped image can be resized. Resizing should preserve the cropping and the cropped aspect ratio.
